meta.decision_rationale: Brand DNA synthesizes library's essential identity from scenarios and competitive position. Guides all downstream governance and collection decisions.
evidence_refs: Phase 1 scenarios (esp. Civic Hub preferred, Politicized feared)
Community survey data on values (equity, accessibility, intellectual freedom)
tradeoffs: Explicit value statements may alienate some stakeholders
Committing to values in writing creates accountability

payload.tagline: Knowledge, trust, and community voice at the center

core_values.definition: We protect the right to seek, share, and challenge ideas—without censorship or algorithmic control.
core_values.governance_implication: Collection decisions made by community, not corporate algorithms or political pressure

core_values.definition: We ensure everyone—regardless of income, education, or ability—can access knowledge and community.
core_values.governance_implication: Free access, materials in multiple formats, staff trained in accessibility

core_values.definition: We are governed by and accountable to the community we serve, not distant corporate or political entities.
core_values.governance_implication: Formal governance structure with community representation; transparent decision-making

core_values.definition: We believe unexpected encounters with books, people, and ideas enrich life in ways algorithms can't replicate.
core_values.governance_implication: Diverse collection curated by humans, community curation alongside expert curation

payload.brand_archetype: The Mentor / Sage (trusted guide to knowledge) + The Everyman (for everyone, no gatekeepers)

tone_voice.tone: Warm, accessible, intellectually serious but not elitist
voice_elements: We speak directly, not in jargon
We admit what we don't know
We center community perspective, not expert authority
